     3|## Critique of the current fix
     4|
     5|The port-clearing loop added to `launch` is a band-aid with four structural problems:
     6|
     7|1. **Reactive, not preventive** -- it only kills stale processes on re-launch, not when they become stale.
     8|2. **No shutdown path** -- Docker runs forever (`restart: unless-stopped`). There is no user-facing way to stop the dashboard short of running `docker compose down` manually.
     9|3. **Invisible state** -- after launch the script exits; nothing shows the user that the dashboard is running. No status bar icon, no Dock presence.
    10|4. **Two disconnected systems** -- [menubar_app.py](menubar_app.py) provides proper lifecycle (status bar + quit) but runs a local server. The [launch](Experiment Tracker.app/Contents/MacOS/launch) script uses Docker but has zero lifecycle management. They conflict on port 8765.
    11|

    13|
    14|```mermaid
    15|flowchart TD
    16|    UserClick["User opens Experiment Tracker.app"] --> AppleScript["osacompile wrapper"]
    17|    AppleScript --> Launch["bash launch script"]
    18|    Launch --> KillStale["Kill stale port holders"]
    19|    KillStale --> StartDocker["docker compose up -d --build"]
    20|    StartDocker --> WaitReady["Wait for :8765 ready"]
    21|    WaitReady --> Menubar["python3 menubar_app.py --docker"]

    26|        Icon --> OpenCursor["Open in Cursor"]
    27|        Icon --> QuitBtn["Quit"]
    28|    end
    29|
    30|    QuitBtn --> DockerDown["docker compose down"]
    31|    DockerDown --> Exit["Process exits, icon disappears"]
    32|    OpenDash --> Browser["Opens/focuses browser tab"]
    33|```
